stories.title: CPSC Children's Sleepwear Recall Wave — Blossom, Silks, Andy & Evan: Burn Hazard Alert

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
RECALL ALERT: 3 children's sleepwear brands pulled for burn hazard.

Blossom Blanks, Silks, Andy & Evan — all violate federal flammability standards.

Stop using now. Cut in half, contact brand for refund.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
COS parents, it's time to check your kids' pajama drawers.

Three children's sleepwear brands have been recalled by the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission for violating mandatory flammability standards — meaning they can catch fire more quickly than the law allows, creating a burn hazard for children.

Here's what you need to know about each:

**Blossom Blanks (Blossom)** — About 630 two-piece loungewear sets in sizes 12M through 12Y are recalled. The sets were sold at children's boutiques and online between September 2023 and May 2025. If you have one, stop using it immediately, cut it in half, photograph the destroyed garment, and email the photo to sales@blossomocc.com for a full refund.

**Silks** — Two-piece loungewear sets in sizes 2T through 13/14 are recalled. These were sold at silksdesign.com from January 2024 through September 2025 for $40-$50. Stop using, cut in half, and email a photo to silkssupport@silksdesign.com for a refund.

**Andy & Evan** — Children's pajama sets that also violate mandatory flammability standards. Contact Andy & Evan directly for remedy details.

No injuries have been reported for any of these products — but the recall is precautionary and serious. Children's sleepwear flammability standards exist specifically because kids move around, roll near lights or candles, and need that protection built into their clothing.

Do you check labels before buying kids' PJs? What's your go-to for finding safe sleepwear?

---

#### Image Caption -- 8:00 AM MT
facebook.image_caption: Three children's sleepwear brands recalled for burn hazard: Blossom Blanks, Silks, and Andy & Evan. Stop using immediately and contact the brand for a refund. Check your kids' PJ drawer today.

article.headline: CPSC Recall Alert: Three Children's Sleepwear Brands Pulled for Burn Hazard — What COS Families Need to Do Now

stories.title: D49 2026 Graduation: Full Schedule for Colorado Springs Families

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
D49 Graduation 2026 dates are set.

May 15: PPEC, Springs Studio, Patriot (COS City Hub)
May 23: Sand Creek, Falcon, Vista Ridge (Broadmoor World Arena)

Full schedule at d49.org — some programs TBD.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
D49 families — it's officially graduation season.

District 49 has released its 2026 graduation schedule, and there are two big ceremony days to mark on your calendar.

On Friday, May 15, three programs will hold ceremonies at the COS City Hub (4304 Austin Bluffs Pkwy):
- PPEC: 8:30 AM
- Springs Studio for Academic Excellence: 11:30 AM
- Patriot Applied Learning Campus: 3:00 PM

On Saturday, May 23, three high schools will hold ceremonies at the Broadmoor World Arena (3185 Venetucci Blvd):
- Sand Creek High School: 9:00 AM
- Falcon High School: 1:00 PM
- Vista Ridge High School: 5:00 PM

Families waiting for Liberty Tree Academy, Banning Lewis Preparatory Academy, and Power Technical Early Colleges — those dates are still coming. Keep checking d49.org for updates.

If your family's graduating a senior this spring, we're thinking of you. It's been quite a journey.

What's your family's graduation tradition — big dinner, a trip, or something else entirely?

---

#### Image Caption -- 10:00 AM MT
facebook.image_caption: D49 graduation 2026: May 15 at COS City Hub and May 23 at Broadmoor World Arena. Full schedule at d49.org — some programs TBD.

stories.title: Mother's Day 2026 COS: 4 Family-Friendly Plans to Book Now (May 10)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Mother's Day is May 10 — book now, COS.

Royal Gorge trains, Gather Mountain Blooms garden party, Wolf Center wolf tours, Broadmoor brunch.

Something for every family and every budget.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Mother's Day is two weeks away, and Colorado Springs has some genuinely special options for families who want to make it memorable.

Here's a quick rundown of what's available — and a heads-up that some of these book up fast:

**Royal Gorge Route Railroad Mother's Day Trains (May 9-10)** — A 2-hour scenic excursion through the Royal Gorge with gourmet dining or casual menu options. Departures from 9 AM to 6:30 PM. One of the most consistently loved Mother's Day outings in the area.

**Gather Mountain Blooms Garden Party (May 9-10, 10 AM-12:30 PM)** — Charcuterie brunch, hands-on garden craft, and flower starters to take home. Pricing: $50 per adult, $40 per child 12 and under. A beautiful outdoor experience.

**Colorado Wolf & Wildlife Center (May 10, 9-11 AM)** — A wolf tour with a flower for every mom in attendance. $40 for adults 12 and older, $20 for kids ages 6-11. Educational and genuinely memorable for kids.

**The Broadmoor Hall Mother's Day Brunch (May 10, 10 AM-3 PM)** — The classic Colorado Springs splurge. $135 per adult, $50 per child. Reservations required — and they will fill up.

We're only two weeks out. If you've been thinking about any of these, now's the time to book.

Which Mother's Day tradition does your family swear by?

---

#### Image Caption -- 12:00 PM MT
facebook.image_caption: Mother's Day is May 10 — and Colorado Springs has plenty of family-friendly plans worth booking now. From Royal Gorge trains to garden parties to wolf tours, there's something for every family.

stories.title: MeadowGrass Music Festival 2026: Kids Under 12 Free, Memorial Day Weekend

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Kids 12 and under are free at MeadowGrass Music Festival this year.

May 22-24, Memorial Day Weekend, La Foret in Black Forest.

Kid Zone, camping, live music, nature hikes. This one's worth the drive.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you're looking for a Memorial Day Weekend plan that the whole family will talk about for years, MeadowGrass might be it.

The 17th Annual MeadowGrass Music Festival runs May 22-24 at La Foret Conference & Retreat Center in Black Forest — just north of Colorado Springs. It's a three-day outdoor music and camping festival with a genuine family-first vibe.

Here's the kid highlight: children 12 and under get in free with a paid adult.

The Faricy Boys Kid Zone is packed with hands-on activities: giant bubbles from Milibo Art Theater, crafts from Concrete Couch, a hay wagon, kid yoga, and art. There are also guided nature hikes and music workshops. Camping is available in the Ponderosa Forest if you want to make it a full weekend adventure.

This year's music lineup includes Kyle Hollingsworth Band, Nershi Hann Trio, ALO, and Shook Twins — a solid mix if you like Americana and bluegrass.

It's a lot to pack into one weekend, and families who've been tend to come back year after year.

Has your family done MeadowGrass before? What's your favorite part of the festival?

---

#### Image Caption -- 3:00 PM MT
facebook.image_caption: MeadowGrass Music Festival returns May 22-24 at La Foret in Black Forest. Kids 12 and under are free with a paid adult. Camping, Kid Zone, and live music — right in the Pikes Peak region.

article.headline: MeadowGrass Music Festival 2026: Kids Under 12 Free, Memorial Day Weekend in Black Forest

stories.title: Touch-A-Truck May 16: Free Family Fun at 1045 Lower Gold Camp Rd

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Free family alert: Touch-A-Truck is Saturday, May 16.

9:30 AM at 1045 Lower Gold Camp Rd in Colorado Springs.

Big trucks, fire engines, construction vehicles — and it's completely free.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Looking for a free Saturday activity that the little ones will absolutely love?

Touch-A-Truck is coming to Colorado Springs on Saturday, May 16, starting at 9:30 AM at 1045 Lower Gold Camp Rd.

If you've never been to a Touch-A-Truck event, here's the idea: kids get up close and hands-on with the big vehicles they love — fire trucks, construction equipment, police cars, and more. They can climb up, sit in the cab, honk the horn, and explore with real workers and volunteers on hand to answer questions.

It's completely free. No registration required. Just show up with your kids and enjoy a morning exploring the machines that keep Colorado Springs running.

These events tend to draw a crowd, so arriving early is always a good move.

Will you be bringing your kids to Touch-A-Truck this year? What kind of trucks are your kids most excited to see?

---

#### Image Caption -- 5:00 PM MT
facebook.image_caption: Touch-A-Truck is Saturday, May 16 at 9:30 AM — 1045 Lower Gold Camp Rd in Colorado Springs. Free for all families. Fire trucks, construction vehicles, police cars, and more.
